title: express-session with cookie.sameSite: "none" — the session cookie is sent on cross-site requests (CSRF)

params.passDetail: express-session sets cookie.sameSite to a restrictive value ("lax" or "strict", not "none") — the session cookie is not sent on cross-site requests, blocking CSRF.
params.failDetail: express-session is configured with cookie: { sameSite: "none" }, so the session cookie is attached to cross-site requests. This removes the SameSite CSRF defense: a malicious page can trigger state-changing requests that carry the victim's session. Unless a true cross-site flow is required (and paired with CSRF tokens), use sameSite: "lax" or "strict".
params.absentCallDetail: This scope does not call session(); this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: session() does not set cookie.sameSite; verify the effective value is lax/strict, not none.

provenance.note: sameSite: "none" is set to support an embedded/cross-site widget and applied to the whole session, silently disabling the SameSite CSRF defense for every state-changing route.
